it.cellula_subtitle: Unità strutturale e funzionale fondamentale di tutti gli organismi viventi.
it.cellula_description_part1: Per assolvere alle proprie funzioni fisiologiche, le cellule si organizzano in tessuti, i quali cooperano per costituire strutture a complessità crescente:
it.cellula_description_part2: Il corpo umano è composto da centinaia di tipi cellulari differenziati, ciascuno essenziale per l'omeostasi e la salute dell'organismo. Queste cellule specializzate coordinano i processi vitali, consentendo l'attività contrattile del miocardio, i processi cognitivi ed elettrici cerebrali, la filtrazione ematica renale e la rigenerazione continua del tessuto epiteliale.
it.cellula_mitosi_part1: Tutte queste cellule somatiche originano da cellule preesistenti attraverso un processo controllato di divisione cellulare denominato
it.cellula_mitosi_part2: , che garantisce la trasmissione fedele del patrimonio genetico a due cellule figlie geneticamente identiche.
it.life_basis_title: LA CELLULA COME BASE DELLA VITA
it.life_basis_intro_p1: Gli organismi viventi sono tutti costituiti da
it.life_basis_intro_p2: , organizzate sostanzialmente nello stesso modo. L’insieme di studi al microscopio e le osservazioni di numerosi ricercatori permisero di arrivare alla
it.cell_theory_point1: 1) tutti i viventi sono formati da una o più cellule;
it.cell_theory_point2: 2) le cellule costituiscono le unità fondamentali di ciascun organismo;
it.cell_theory_point3: 3) tutte le cellule derivano da altre cellule.
it.organisms_classification_p1: Tutti i viventi sono costituiti da una o più cellule: in base a questa caratteristica possono essere suddivisi, rispettivamente, in organismi
it.organisms_classification_enfasi_uni: archeobatteri, eubatteri, cianobatteri e gran parte dei protisti
it.organisms_classification_enfasi_pluri: le piante, gli animali e i funghi pluricellulari.
it.cell_types_intro_p1: In natura esistono due tipi di cellule: la
it.cell_types_intro_enfasi: cellula eucariote e la cellula procariote.

it.component1_description_enfasi_reazioni: avvengono le vie metaboliche e le reazioni chimiche necessarie all'omeostasi cellulare e alla replicazione;
it.component2_description_enfasi_garantisce: regola gli scambi e la comunicazione con l’ambiente extracellulare;
it.component3_description_enfasi_programma: codice informativo per lo sviluppo, il differenziamento, l'attività metabolica e la riproduzione.

it.mitosis_intro: La proliferazione cellulare avviene per divisione equazionale mitotica: ciascuna cellula madre si scisce originando due cellule figlie mediante il processo di
it.mitosis_result: Questo meccanismo produce due cellule figlie geneticamente stabili, speculari alla cellula madre per morfologia e corredo genico. Per garantire ciò, la cellula attua una fedele replicazione semiconservativa del proprio DNA nucleare ed una equa ripartizione (citodieresi) degli organuli, delle membrane e delle componenti citoplasmatiche ereditate dalla cellula madre.
it.cell_types_mitosis_cellule_somatiche: cellule somatiche attive nel ciclo cellulare
it.cell_types_mitosis_p3: , sebbene il potenziale replicativo vari a seconda del tessuto. Specifiche linee cellulari indifferenziate mantengono una capacità di autorinnovamento asimmetrico illimitato per tutta la vita, potendo differenziarsi in linee specializzate: tali elementi sono definiti
it.meiosis_intro: La linea germinale segue invece una via di divisione differente, esclusiva delle cellule sessuali destinate alla gametogenesi (spermatozoi e ovociti): la
it.meiosis_definition_p4: va incontro a due round di segregazione cromosomica successivi alla replicazione del DNA, generando gameti provvisti di corredo
it.meiosis_result_p3: . A differenza della mitosi, la meiosi dimezza la ploidia cromosomica e introduce variabilità genetica; durante la profase I, infatti, l'appaiamento dei cromosomi omologhi consente il
it.meiosis_result_scambio_ricombinazione: scambio fisico di segmenti di DNA cromatidico
it.meiosis_result_evoluzione: evoluzione biologica e della variabilità fenotipica degli organismi eucariotici
it.sexual_reproduction_intro_ricombinazione: fusione e il riassortimento indipendente dei gameti
it.sexual_reproduction_intro_p4: derivanti da due individui parentali geneticamente distinti generano una progenie caratterizzata da nuove combinazioni alleliche stabili.
it.fertilization_process_p1: Ciascun gamete parentale apporta un set cromosomico aploide
it.fertilization_process_p5: ripristina lo stato diploide (2n) del cariotipo, originando una singola cellula totipotente denominata
it.fertilization_process_p6: , da cui si svilupperà l'intero organismo pluricellulare.
it.cell_nucleus_description: Organulo citoplasmatico delimitato da un involucro nucleare a doppia membrana provvisto di complessi del poro per il trasporto nucleo-citoplasmatico selettivo,
it.cell_nucleus_content_dna_rna: sede del genoma (DNA) e dei processi di replicazione e trascrizione (RNA).
it.mitochondrion_energy_production: Produzione di energia metabolica chimica (ATP) attraverso la β-ossidazione, il ciclo di Krebs e la fosforilazione ossidativa nella catena di trasporto degli elettroni.
it.golgi_apparatus_description: Compartimento membranoso polarizzato adibito alla maturazione e modificazione post-traduzionale (O-glicosilazione e N-glicosilazione complessa di proteine e lipidi);
it.golgi_apparatus_function: smista ed indirizza i flussi vescicolari provenienti dal reticolo endoplasmatico verso la membrana plasmatica (secrezione) o verso la via endo-lisosomiale.
it.endoplasmic_reticulum_description1_p1: Rete interconnessa di cisterne e tubuli integrata nel
it.er_smooth_function_p1: Il dominio LISCIO (tubulare, privo di ribosomi) coordina la
it.er_smooth_function_yellow: biosintesi dei lipidi, la sintesi di ormoni steroidei
it.er_smooth_function_rest: e funge da principale sito di immagazzinamento e
it.er_smooth_function_regolazione: rilascio controllato di ioni Calcio per la segnalazione cellulare
it.er_smooth_detox_p1: Nel fegato esprime complessi enzimatici (come il citocromo P450) per la
it.er_smooth_detox_yellow: detossificazione di composti idrofobici e xenobiotici e per la sintesi del colesterolo
it.er_rough_rer_p1: Il dominio RUGOSO presenta ribosomi ancorati sulle membrane ed è la sede d'elezione per la
it.er_rough_rer_p2: delle catene polipeptidiche destinate alle vie di
it.er_rough_function: sintesi proteica transmembrana e di secrezione.
it.peroxisome_functions: Sito chiave per la catabolizzazione e β-ossidazione degli acidi grassi a catena molto lunga (VLCFA), biosintesi dei plasmalogeni essenziali per le guaine mieliniche neuronali e i tensioattivi polmonari. Produce perossido di idrogeno (H<sub>2</sub>O<sub>2</sub>) come sottoprodotto ossidativo, prontamente neutralizzato dall'enzima catalasi interno per prevenire lo stress ossidativo. Interviene nelle tappe iniziali della sintesi degli acidi biliari epatici
it.lysosome_degradation_function: Organulo deputato alla degradazione enzimatica controllata di macromolecole endogene ed esogene internalizzate per endocitosi o fagocitosi.
it.lysosome_recycling: Coinvolto nell'eliminazione di detriti cellulari e nella clearance di agenti patogeni internalizzati dai macrofagi e neutrofili del sistema immunitario.
it.lysosome_autophagy_heterophagy: Mediatore delle vie di Autofagia ed Eterofagia per il turnover e riciclo degli organuli danneggiati.
it.lysosome_autophagosome: Fusione con l'autofagosoma per scindere i componenti strutturali deteriorati e convertire i monomeri molecolari in fonti di energia metabolica.
it.dna_description1_p1: Acido desossiribonucleico, il supporto chimico permanente dell'
it.dna_description2_p3: , governando l'espressione dei caratteri biochimici e morfologici. I nucleotidi (monomeri costitutivi) sono formati da un gruppo fosfato, uno zucchero desossiribosio e una base azotata purinica o pirimidinica.
it.dna_imprinting_function: Il DNA regola trascrizionalmente l'espressione genica differenziata, determinando i profili di programmazione funzionale (Geni) in base ai quali i vari componenti cellulari attuano le proprie risposte biologiche specializzate
it.genes_description_p2: corrispondenti a specifiche sequenze di DNA trascritte in molecole di RNA funzionale o in RNA messaggeri destinati alla traduzione in una
it.genes_protein_production: L'espressione controllata di questi prodotti genici è strettamente necessaria alla sopravvivenza, proliferazione, omeostasi e differenziamento specifico della cellula.
it.chromosomes_description_p2: Strutture nucleoproteiche costituite da lunghi filamenti di DNA stabilizzati e avvolti attorno a core istonici; si condensano dinamicamente per consentire la corretta segregazione genica durante le fasi mitotiche e meiotiche.
it.tissues_definition: Un tessuto è un livello di organizzazione biologica composto da un consorzio di cellule specializzate aventi origine embrionale, caratteristiche morfologiche e funzioni simili, integrate e coordinate per mezzo di una matrice extracellulare (ECM) di composizione chimica e consistenza altamente variabile.
it.tissues_list_intro: I quattro tessuti biologici fondamentali del corpo umano sono:
it.epithelial_tissue_definition_cellule_add: cellule strettamente adese e polarizzate strutturalmente
it.epithelial_tissue_definition_spazi_ridot: matrice extracellulare intercellulare è ridotta a sottili fessure
it.epithelial_cell_connections_p1: La coesione meccanica dell'epitelio è assicurata da specializzazioni della membrana plasmatica laterale che formano giunzioni ancoranti, occludenti e comunicanti, le quali si organizzano nel
it.epithelial_cell_connections_giunzionale: complesso giunzionale transmembrana stabilizzato dal citoscheletro
it.epithelia_types_intro_p1: Si classificano in tre categorie funzionali:
it.epithelia_types_intro_p3: (specializzati nell'attività secretoria esocrina o endocrina) e
it.epithelia_types_intro_p4: (cellule epiteliali modificate per la ricezione di stimoli, es. gemme gustative).
it.epithelium_connective_relationship_intro: Il tessuto epiteliale poggia costantemente sul
, a cui è ancorato tramite l'interposizione della
(struttura acellulare complessa ricca di collagene di tipo IV e laminina). Poiché il tessuto epiteliale è
it.epithelium_connective_relationship_privo: completamente avascolare (privo di vasi sanguigni propri)
it.epithelium_connective_relationship_intro: , lo scambio di ossigeno e nutrienti avviene esclusivamente per diffusione passiva attraverso la membrana basale a partire dai letti capillari del connettivo.
it.covering_epithelia_layers_intro: Gli epiteli di rivestimento vengono classificati citologicamente in base alla morfologia cellulare e al numero di strati:
it.simple_epithelium_details: , composto da un unico strato cellulare poggiante sulla membrana basale;
it.compound_epithelium_details: , costituito da molteplici strati sovrapposti di cellule, in cui solo lo strato basale tocca la membrana;
it.pseudostratified_epithelium_details: , variante monostratificata in cui tutte le cellule toccano la base ma, avendo altezze e posizioni nucleari differenti, simulano un assetto pluristratificato alla sezione istologica.
it.epithelial_functions_intro_p1: Essi rivestono la superficie esterna del corpo (l’
it.epithelial_functions_intro_p2: , tessuto epiteliale cheratinizzato della cute), i canali e gli organi cavi comunicanti con l’esterno (formando l'epitelio della
it.epithelial_functions_intro_p3: ), i lumi dei vasi sanguigni e linfatici (costituendo l’
it.epithelial_functions_intro_p4: ), e le cavità sierose chiuse interne ed organi associati (costituendo il
it.epithelial_functions_intro_p5: protettivo di pleura, pericardio e peritoneo).
it.factors_determining_function: Il differenziamento citologico, il numero degli strati e la specifica localizzazione anatomica condizionano strettamente le proprietà funzionali dell'epitelio (protezione meccanica, assorbimento selettivo, secrezione, barriera chimico-fisica o transito molecolare).
it.glandular_epithelia_definition_p1: Gli epiteli ghiandolari sono formati da cellule specializzate nell'anabolismo e nella secrezione regolata di sostanze biologicamente attive, definite
it.glandular_epithelia_definition_p2: . Tali cellule possono operare come elementi unicellulari secretori isolati o organizzarsi in parenchimi complessi che strutturano le
it.gland_classification_intro: Le ghiandole vengono suddivise e classificate principalmente in base alla modalità, alla morfologia e alla via di estrusione del secreto:
it.gland_classification_by_secretion: In base alla topografia del rilascio del secreto, si distinguono in:
it.exocrine_glands_details: , che riversano il secreto sulla superficie corporea esterna o nel lume di un organo cavo, avvalendosi di
it.endocrine_glands_details: , prive di dotti escretori, che rilasciano i propri messaggeri chimici, definiti
it.endocrine_glands_details_p2: , direttamente nello spazio interstiziale affinché diffondano nel torrente ematico verso organi bersaglio distanti.
it.connective_tissue_definition_p1: Il tessuto connettivo è istologicamente caratterizzato dalla prevalenza volumetrica di
it.connective_tissue_definition_p2: rispetto alla frazione cellulare. L'ECM è composta da una
it.connective_tissue_definition_p3: (idrogel ricco di acqua, glicosaminoglicani, proteoglicani e glicoproteine strutturali) nella quale sono immerse le cellule e tre principali varietà di
it.connective_tissue_definition_fibre_tipi: fibre collagene (resistenza alla trazione), fibre reticolari (impalcatura) e fibre elastiche (conformazione reversibile)
it.connective_tissue_subdivisions_intro: In base alla densità, alla tipologia cellulare e alla composizione biochimica della matrice e delle fibre, il tessuto connettivo si differenzia in:
it.connective_tissue_list_1: tessuto connettivo propriamente detto (lasso o denso);
it.connective_tissue_list_2: tessuto cartilagineo (matrice gelificata solida ma flessibile);
it.connective_tissue_functions_intro: Il tessuto connettivo adempie a fondamentali ruoli omeostatici e strutturali:
it.connective_function_1: connessione strutturale e coesione metabolica tra tessuti e organi differenti del corpo;
it.connective_function_2: sostegno meccanico dell’organismo (impalcatura scheletrica e cartilaginea) e protezione degli organi vitali racchiusi nelle cavità corporee;
it.connective_function_3: mediazione degli scambi trofici e trasporto di metaboliti, assicurando la nutrizione dei tessuti epiteliali avascolari;
it.connective_function_4: risposta immunitaria e barriera difensiva biologica contro patogeni ed infezioni (tramite leucociti residenti o migranti).
it.connective_function_summary: In sintesi, esplica funzioni trofiche, meccaniche di sostegno, protezione e difesa biologica.
it.adipose_tissue_definition_p1: È una forma specializzata di connettivo lasso, composto prevalentemente da cellule denominate
it.adipose_tissue_definition_p2: , deputate all'accumulo endocellulare di lipidi (principalmente trigliceridi) sotto forma di
it.adipose_tissue_types_p1: Si classifica in due tipologie distinte: tessuto adiposo
it.adipose_tissue_types_p2: . Il tessuto bianco funge da principale riserva energetica dell'organismo, isolante termico e organo endocrino; il tessuto bruno è specializzato nella termogenesi senza brivido, dissipando il gradiente protonico mitocondriale in energia termica (calore) grazie all'espressione della proteina UCP-1 (termojenina).
it.nervous_tissue_definition_p1: Il tessuto nervoso è un sistema altamente integrato composto da cellule eccitabili e filamenti associati. Le unità funzionali primarie sono i
it.nervous_tissue_definition_p3: , capaci di generare, modulare e trasmettere potenziali d'azione in risposta a stimoli specifici. Il tessuto comprende anche la neuroglia (cellule gliali), essenziale per il supporto metabolico e strutturale dei neuroni.
it.neuron_structure_p1: I neuroni sono formati da un corpo cellulare (soma o pericario) contenente il nucleo, da cui si dipartono prolungamenti afferenti ramificati detti
it.neuron_structure_p2: e un singolo prolungamento efferente, generalmente lungo e mielinizzato, denominato
it.neuron_association: I fasci di assoni associati a tessuti connettivi di rivestimento strutturano i nervi del sistema periferico.
it.neuron_reproduction_note_p1: I neuroni maturi sono cellule per lo più perenni entrate stabilmente in fase G<sub>0</sub> del ciclo cellulare; tuttavia, la ricerca scientifica moderna ha dimostrato l'esistenza di fenomeni di
it.neuron_reproduction_note_non_si_riproduc: neurogenesi adulta a partire da cellule staminali neurali confinate in specifiche nicchie cerebrali (come la zona sottogranulare dell'ippocampo)
it.neuron_properties_intro: La cellula nervosa elabora e propaga gli impulsi bioelettrici basandosi su due proprietà fondamentali:
it.neuron_property_excitability_details: , ossia la capacità di variare il potenziale di membrana a riposo in risposta a stimoli chimico-fisici, convertendoli in segnali elettrici;
it.neuron_property_conductivity_details: , vale a dire l'abilità di propagare attivamente il potenziale d'azione lungo la membrana assonica senza decremento di segnale.
it.synapse_description_p1: La trasmissione unidirezionale dell'informazione tra neuroni o tra un neurone e una cellula effettrice avviene a livello di giunzioni specializzate dette
it.synapse_description_p2: . Nelle sinapsi chimiche, l'arrivo del potenziale d'azione induce l'esocitosi di molecole di
it.synapse_description_p3: nello spazio sinaptico. Legandosi ai recettori sulla membrana postsinaptica, queste molecole innescano potenziali postsinaptici che avviano, inibiscono o modulano l'impulso nervoso, coordinando l'attività integrativa dell'organismo.
it._section_dyslexia: === Versione semplice per dislessia e ipovisione  ===
it.cell_definition: La cellula è l'unità più piccola di ogni essere vivente. È come un mattone che costruisce il corpo.
it.cell_hierarchy_1: Le cellule si uniscono per formare i tessuti.
it.cell_hierarchy_3: Gli organi lavorano insieme in apparati e sistemi.
it.cell_importance: Il corpo umano ha tantissimi tipi di cellule. Sono tutte importanti per stare bene.
it.cell_functions_intro: Le cellule fanno funzionare il corpo. Grazie a loro:
it.cell_reproduction: Ogni cellula nasce da un’altra cellula. Si divide in due cellule identiche. Questo processo si chiama
it.simple_nucleus_description_1: È il "centro di controllo" della cellula. Ha una doppia membrana con piccoli fori (pori) per comunicare con il resto.
it.simple_nucleus_description_2: Dentro ci sono il DNA e l’RNA, che contengono le informazioni genetiche.
it.simple_mitochondrion_description: È la "centrale energetica". Produce energia sotto forma di ATP, che serve a tutte le funzioni cellulari.
it.simple_golgi_apparatus_description_1: Prepara, modifica e spedisce proteine e grassi.
it.simple_golgi_apparatus_description_2: È come un "ufficio postale" della cellula. Riceve proteine dal reticolo endoplasmatico e le manda ai lisosomi o alla membrana.
it.simple_er_intro: È un sistema di tubi nel citoplasma. Ha due tipi:
it.simple_smooth_er_description: Ha la superficie liscia perché non ha ribosomi.
it.simple_smooth_er_function_3: Aiuta il fegato a pulire il sangue (detossificazione)
it.simple_smooth_er_function_4: Regola la quantità di calcio nella cellula
it.simple_rough_er_description_1: Ha la superficie "rugosa" perché è ricoperto di ribosomi.
it.simple_rough_er_description_2: I ribosomi servono per produrre proteine. Questo processo si chiama sintesi proteica.
it.simple_peroxisome_description_intro: È una piccola struttura chiusa da una membrana. Ha funzioni importanti:
it.simple_peroxisome_function_1: Produce acidi biliari, che aiutano a digerire i grassi
it.simple_peroxisome_function_2: Favorisce l’assorbimento di vitamine come la A e la K
it.simple_peroxisome_function_3: Produce acqua ossigenata per difendersi dai batteri
it.simple_peroxisome_function_5: Produce sostanze (plasmalogeni) utili per cervello e polmoni
it.simple_lysosome_description_1: È la "discarica" e il "riciclatore" della cellula.
it.simple_lysosome_function_2: Ricicla le parti vecchie della cellula (autofagia)
it.simple_lysosome_function_3: Aiuta i globuli bianchi a combattere le infezioni
it.simple_lysosome_description_2: Grazie ai lisosomi, la cellula si pulisce e si rigenera.
it.simple_dna_definition: Il DNA è la molecola che contiene le informazioni genetiche di ogni persona.
it.simple_dna_components_intro: È fatto di tanti pezzetti chiamati nucleotidi. Ogni nucleotide ha:
it.simple_dna_component_3: Una base azotata (A, T, C, G)
it.simple_dna_genetic_code: La sequenza di queste basi forma il codice genetico, che decide come è fatto e come funziona il nostro corpo.
it.simple_dna_instructions: Il DNA dà istruzioni alle altre parti della cellula per fare le proteine di cui abbiamo bisogno.
it.simple_genes_definition_intro: Un gene è un pezzo di DNA che contiene le istruzioni per fare:
it.simple_protein_function_3: La sua specializzazione (es. cellula muscolare, nervosa, ecc.)
it.simple_chromosomes_definition: I cromosomi sono strutture a forma di bastoncino fatte di DNA e proteine. Si trovano nel nucleo della cellula.
it.simple_chromosomes_mitosis: Durante la divisione cellulare (mitosi), i cromosomi si copiano e si spostano per dare a ogni nuova cellula lo stesso corredo genetico.
it.simple_chromosomes_human_number: Gli esseri umani hanno 46 cromosomi: 23 ereditati dalla mamma e 23 dal papà.
it.simple_chromosomes_uniqueness: I cromosomi contengono tutti i geni che ci rendono unici: dall’aspetto fisico alle funzioni del corpo.

en.cellula_subtitle: Fundamental structural and functional unit of all living organisms.
en.cellula_description_part1: To perform their physiological functions, cells organize into tissues, which cooperate to form structures of increasing complexity:
en.cellula_description_part2: The human body is composed of hundreds of differentiated cell types, each essential for organism homeostasis and health. These specialized cells coordinate vital processes, enabling myocardial contractile activity, cerebral cognitive and electrical processes, renal blood filtration, and continuous epithelial tissue regeneration.
en.cellula_mitosi_part1: All these somatic cells originate from pre-existing cells through a controlled cell division process called
en.cellula_mitosi_part2: , which ensures the faithful transmission of genetic heritage to two genetically identical daughter cells.
en.life_basis_title: THE CELL AS THE BASIS OF LIFE
en.life_basis_intro_p1: Living organisms are all made up of
en.life_basis_intro_p2: , organized in substantially the same way. The combination of microscopic studies and observations by numerous researchers led to the
en.cell_theory_point1: 1) all living things are formed by one or more cells;
en.cell_theory_point2: 2) cells constitute the fundamental units of each organism;
en.cell_theory_point3: 3) all cells derive from other cells.
en.organisms_classification_p1: All living things are made up of one or more cells: based on this characteristic, they can be divided, respectively, into
en.organisms_classification_enfasi_uni: archaebacteria, eubacteria, cyanobacteria, and most protists
en.cell_types_intro_p1: In nature, there are two types of cells: the
en.cell_types_intro_enfasi: eukaryotic cell and the prokaryotic cell.
en.cell_components_intro: In order to carry out their many activities, all cells must have:
en.component1_description_enfasi_reazioni: metabolic pathways and chemical reactions necessary for cellular homeostasis and replication take place;
en.component2_description_enfasi_separa: separates the cytoplasm from the surrounding environment
en.component2_description_enfasi_garantisce: regulates its exchanges and communication with the extracellular environment;
en.component3_description_enfasi_programma: information code for development, differentiation, metabolic activity, and reproduction.
en.eukaryotes_prokaryotes_comparison_p1: These three fundamental components are found in both prokaryotic and eukaryotic cells: the former characterize
en.eukaryotes_prokaryotes_comparison_enfasi: protists, fungi, plants, and animals (including humans)
en.eukaryotes_prokaryotes_comparison_p3: . Prokaryotes and eukaryotes differ profoundly in
en.eukaryotes_prokaryotes_comparison_enfasi: plasma membrane based on the same principle of the phospholipid bilayer
en.eukaryotic_details_description_p2: , as the different metabolic functions are separated into specialized structures, known as intracellular compartments, called
en.eukaryotic_details_description_enfasi_nu: well-defined nucleus enclosed by a double-membrane nuclear envelope
en.eukaryotic_details_description_enfasi_co: protection, replication, and transcription of DNA
en.eukaryotic_details_description_p5: . All higher organisms (animals and plants) are made up of billions of eukaryotic cells. These cells generally have a diameter between
en.eukaryotic_details_description_p6: (the micrometer, μm, corresponds to one millionth of a meter). The other type of cells, called prokaryotic, are
en.eukaryotic_details_description_enfasi_se: smaller (with average sizes in the range of 1-5 μm) and structurally simpler
en.eukaryotic_details_description_p7: . The main difference lies in the
en.eukaryotic_details_description_p8: , so the genetic material is concentrated in a region called the nucleoid, directly
en.eukaryotic_details_description_p9: . They also lack membranous organelles, with the exception of
en.eukaryotic_details_description_enfasi_ri: ribosomes, macromolecular complexes responsible for protein synthesis
en.eukaryotic_details_description_p10: . Metabolic functions are nonetheless ensured by enzyme complexes associated with the membrane or dissolved in the cytosol. All bacteria consist of a single prokaryotic cell (unicellular organisms).
en.cytoplasm_details_p1: Every cell is immersed in a predominantly aqueous extracellular microenvironment. The interior of the cell is occupied by the
en.cytoplasm_details_enfasi_molecole: water, ions, mineral salts, biomolecules, and soluble macrocomplexes
en.cell_membrane_details_p1: To regulate molecular exchanges and prevent the loss of cellular components, there is a stable hydrophobic barrier that separates the internal environment from the external one. This barrier, called
en.fluid_mosaic_model_p2: are amphipathic molecules, composed of a hydrophilic glycerol "head" linked to a phosphate group and two hydrophobic fatty acid "tails"
en.fluid_mosaic_model_p3: . In the membrane structure, both lipids and proteins possess the ability to
en.fluid_mosaic_model_enfasi_muoversi: diffuse laterally (move freely within the layer)
en.fluid_mosaic_model_p4: , giving the barrier a dynamic plasticity known as the "
en.plasma_membrane_permeability_intro_enfas: strictly regulates the entry and exit of chemical substances
preserve cytoplasmic homeostasis and ideal ionic concentrations for biological processes
en.plasma_membrane_permeability_intro_p5: . Most ions and water-soluble or polar molecules cannot spontaneously cross the hydrophobic core of the membrane; to do so, they require specific
en.plasma_membrane_permeability_intro_p6: or integral transmembrane protein complexes that form actual
en.plasma_membrane_recognition_p1: The membrane also constitutes the interface for intercellular molecular recognition.
en.plasma_membrane_recognition_p2: It displays on its extracellular side
en.plasma_membrane_recognition_p3: , often linked to proteins or lipids to form
en.plasma_membrane_recognition_p4: which act as surface markers or "
en.plasma_membrane_recognition_p5: ". This system allows cells to be identified as "self" and avoid attack by the
en.plasma_membrane_recognition_p6: , while structures recognized as foreign ("non-self") are targeted for destruction.
en.cytoskeleton_intro_enfasi_rete: three-dimensional network of protein filaments and microtubules
en.cytoskeleton_function_enfasi_sostiene: provides mechanical support, cell shape, motility, and internal vesicular transport
en.cytoskeleton_function_p2: . The cell operates as a dynamic metabolic system that continuously needs to
en.cytoskeleton_function_enfasi_scambiare: import nutrients and eliminate catabolites and waste products
en.cytoskeleton_function_p3: , processes finely coordinated by the plasma membrane with the support of
en.cytoskeleton_function_enfasi_organuli: various specialized organelles arranged within the internal compartments
en.endoplasmic_reticulum_description_p1: , an interconnected system of tubules, cisternae, and folded membranes that extends from the nuclear envelope and is organized into
en.endoplasmic_reticulum_description_enfasi: two morphologically and functionally distinct regions
), lacking associated ribosomes, is the primary site for membrane lipid biosynthesis, carbohydrate metabolism
synthesis of steroid hormones from cholesterol
en.endoplasmic_reticulum_description_p4: , as well as the storage and
en.endoplasmic_reticulum_description_p5: . In liver cells, the S.E.R. is fundamental in the processes of
en.endoplasmic_reticulum_description_enfasi: detoxification of xenobiotics, drugs, and toxic substances
en.endoplasmic_reticulum_description_p7: ) has its outer surface studded with macromolecular complexes called
en.endoplasmic_reticulum_description_enfasi: ribosomes, and is actively involved in the synthesis, folding, and initial modification of proteins destined for secretion or membranous compartments
en.ribosomes_description_enfasi_subunita: two subunits (large and small) composed of ribosomal RNA (rRNA) and proteins
en.ribosomes_description_p3: , and operate both in association with the R.E.R. membranes and
en.golgi_apparatus_description_nucleus: , typically located near the nucleus, consists of a series of dictyosomes (stacks of flattened membranous cisternae).
en.golgi_apparatus_description_p1: It functions as a station for
en.golgi_apparatus_description_enfasi_racco: sorting, biochemical modification (maturation), and targeting of biomolecules destined for secretory pathways or endosomal compartments
en.golgi_apparatus_description_enfasi_espor: reprocessing via glycosylation, selecting, and packaging products from the endoplasmic reticulum into transport vesicles
en.mitochondria_description_p1: , semi-autonomous organelles that operate as the
en.mitochondria_description_enfasi_energia: chemical energy is generated in the form of adenosine triphosphate (ATP)
en.mitochondria_description_p4: by oxidizing metabolic substrates, such as products derived from glucose
en.mitochondria_description_p5: or fatty acids, consuming molecular oxygen (O<sub>2</sub>). They are distributed throughout the cytoplasm, possess their own genome (mitochondrial DNA), and are enclosed by
en.lysosomes_description_enfasi_struttura: spheroidal organelles enclosed by a single membrane
en.lysosomes_description_enfasi_enzimi: hydrolytic enzymes (acid hydrolases) capable of degrading
en.lysosomes_description_p3: macromolecules, obsolete or damaged cellular organelles, and engulfed pathogens.
en.nucleus_description_p1: constitutes the primary organelle of eukaryotic cells, enclosed by a
en.nucleus_description_p2: , interrupted by transmembrane protein complexes called
en.nucleus_description_enfasi_scambio: selective exchange of macromolecules (such as RNA and proteins) between the nucleoplasm and cytoplasm
en.nucleus_description_p4: . The nucleus encloses within it the
en.nucleus_description_enfasi_acidi: nuclear genome (DNA), RNA molecules, and structural or regulatory proteins
en.nucleus_description_p6: , which associate with DNA strands to
en.nucleus_description_enfasi_compattano: organize them into various levels of structural supercoiling, optimizing their space within the nuclear volume
en.nucleus_description_p7: . This dynamic nucleoprotein complex is called
en.dna_description_p2: whose fundamental repeating unit is the
en.dna_description_p3: . Its three-dimensional structure, described as a
en.dna_description_enfasi_catene: two antiparallel polynucleotide strands wound around a central axis
en.nucleotide_intro: The nucleotides making up each strand consist of:
en.nucleotide_bases: Nitrogenous bases: adenine (A), guanine (G), cytosine (C), thymine (T)
en.dna_bonding_description_p1: Nucleotides link covalently along a single strand via
en.dna_bonding_description_enfasi_legame_zu: phosphodiester bonds between the phosphate group of one nucleotide and the 3' hydroxyl group of the sugar of the next nucleotide
en.dna_bonding_description_p2: . The two complementary strands are held together by hydrogen bonds between the inward-facing nitrogenous bases, following a strict pairing geometry:
en.dna_bonding_description_enfasi_legame_ba: adenine pairs exclusively with thymine, and guanine with cytosine
en.dna_bonding_description_enfasi_basi_impo: linear sequence of these bases constitutes the primary genetic information
en.dna_bonding_description_enfasi_informazi: while the sugar-phosphate backbone provides chemical stability and
en.genetic_code_description_p1: The information encoded within the DNA sequence, read through the nucleotide triplets of the
en.genetic_code_description_enfasi_caratter: morphological, biochemical, and physiological characteristics of the organism
en.genes_proteins_intro_p4: . The specific regions of DNA responsible for the transcription of functional molecules are called
en.genes_proteins_intro_p6: is the minimal informational unit of heredity,
en.genes_proteins_intro_p8: that contains the instructions for the synthesis of a functional RNA (mRNA, tRNA, rRNA) or, through translation, for the
en.genes_proteins_intro_produzione_proteina: synthesis of a specific polypeptide chain (protein)
en.individual_proteins_role_specie: biological species and every single individual
en.individual_proteins_role_responsabili: unique molecular phenotype of the organism
en.individual_proteins_role_p4: and governs the manifestation of different

en.chromosomes_composition_function_p2: are composed of chromatin, which is the organized association of
en.chromosomes_composition_function_p3: , responsible for the hereditary transmission of
en.human_chromosomes_details_p2: houses a total linear length of about
en.human_chromosomes_details_p9: , which are morphologically similar and contain the same gene loci: one is inherited from the
en.haploid_diploid_details_germinali_gameti: germ cells or gametes (sperm and egg cells)
en.haploid_diploid_details_p3: possess a halved chromosome set equal to 23 single-copy chromosomes
en.haploid_diploid_details_p5: compared to the remaining cells of the organism, known as
en.haploid_diploid_details_p7: . For this reason, the set of
en.mitosis_intro: Cellular proliferation occurs through equational mitotic division: each parent cell splits to give rise to two daughter cells through the process of
en.mitosis_result: This mechanism produces two genetically stable daughter cells, mirroring the parent cell in morphology and genetic makeup. To ensure this, the cell carries out a faithful semiconservative replication of its nuclear DNA and an equal distribution (cytokinesis) of organelles, membranes, and cytoplasmic components inherited from the parent cell.
en.cell_types_mitosis_cellule_somatiche: somatic cells active in the cell cycle
en.cell_types_mitosis_p3: , although replicative potential varies depending on the tissue. Specific undifferentiated cell lines maintain an unlimited capacity for asymmetric self-renewal throughout life, capable of differentiating into specialized lineages: these elements are termed
en.meiosis_intro: The germline follows a different division pathway, exclusive to sexual cells destined for gametogenesis (sperm and egg cells):
en.meiosis_definition_p4: undergoes two rounds of chromosome segregation following DNA replication, generating gametes equipped with a
en.meiosis_result_p3: . Unlike mitosis, meiosis halves the chromosomal ploidy and introduces genetic variability; during prophase I, in fact, the pairing of homologous chromosomes allows for
en.meiosis_result_scambio_ricombinazione: physical exchange of chromosomal DNA segments
en.meiosis_result_p6: , a crucial event at the foundation of
en.meiosis_result_evoluzione: biological evolution and phenotypic variability in eukaryotic organisms
en.sexual_reproduction_intro_p2: is the essential preparatory event for
en.sexual_reproduction_intro_ricombinazione: fusion and independent assortment of gametes
en.sexual_reproduction_intro_p4: derived from two genetically distinct parental individuals generate a progeny characterized by new, stable allelic combinations.
en.fertilization_process_p1: Each parental gamete contributes a haploid chromosome set
en.fertilization_process_p5: restores the diploid state (2n) of the karyotype, giving rise to a single totipotent cell called a
en.fertilization_process_p6: , from which the entire multicellular organism will develop.
en.cell_nucleus_description: Cytoplasmic organelle enclosed by a double-membrane nuclear envelope equipped with pore complexes for selective nucleocytoplasmic transport,
en.cell_nucleus_content_dna_rna: the site of the genome (DNA) and of replication and transcription processes (RNA).
en.mitochondrion_energy_production: Production of chemical metabolic energy (ATP) through β-oxidation, the Krebs cycle, and oxidative phosphorylation in the electron transport chain.
en.golgi_apparatus_description: Polarized membranous compartment dedicated to the maturation and post-translational modification (O-glycosylation and complex N-glycosylation of proteins and lipids);
en.golgi_apparatus_function: it sorts and directs vesicular trafficking from the endoplasmic reticulum to the plasma membrane (secretion) or toward the endo-lysosomal pathway.
en.endoplasmic_reticulum_description1_p1: Interconnected network of cisternae and tubules integrated into the
en.er_smooth_function_p1: The SMOOTH domain (tubular, lacking ribosomes) coordinates the
en.er_smooth_function_yellow: biosynthesis of lipids, the synthesis of steroid hormones
en.er_smooth_function_rest: and serves as the primary storage and
en.er_smooth_function_regolazione: controlled release site of Calcium ions for cellular signaling
en.er_smooth_detox_p1: In the liver, it expresses enzyme complexes (such as cytochrome P450) for the
en.er_smooth_detox_yellow: detoxification of hydrophobic and xenobiotic compounds and for cholesterol synthesis
en.er_rough_rer_p1: The ROUGH domain features ribosomes bound to its membranes and is the primary site for the
en.er_rough_rer_p2: of polypeptide chains destined for transmembrane synthesis pathways and secretion.
en.peroxisome_functions: Key site for the catabolism and β-oxidation of very long-chain fatty acids (VLCFA), and biosynthesis of plasmalogens essential for neuronal myelin sheaths and pulmonary surfactants. It produces hydrogen peroxide (H<sub>2</sub>O<sub>2</sub>) as an oxidative byproduct, which is promptly neutralized by the internal catalase enzyme to prevent oxidative stress. It is involved in the initial steps of hepatic bile acid synthesis
en.lysosome_degradation_function: Organelle responsible for the controlled enzymatic degradation of endogenous and exogenous macromolecules internalized by endocytosis or phagocytosis.
en.lysosome_recycling: Involved in the clearance of cellular debris and the elimination of pathogens internalized by macrophages and neutrophils of the immune system.
en.lysosome_autophagy_heterophagy: Mediator of Autophagy and Heterophagy pathways for the turnover and recycling of damaged organelles.
en.lysosome_autophagosome: Fuses with the autophagosome to cleave deteriorated structural components and convert molecular monomers into sources of metabolic energy.
en.dna_description1_p1: Deoxyribonucleic acid, the permanent chemical carrier of
en.dna_description2_p3: , governing the expression of biochemical and morphological traits. Nucleotides (the building block monomers) consist of a phosphate group, a deoxyribose sugar, and a purine or pyrimidine nitrogenous base.
en.dna_imprinting_function: DNA transcriptionally regulates differential gene expression, establishing functional programming profiles (Genes) according to which various cellular components execute their specialized biological responses
en.genes_description_p2: corresponding to specific DNA sequences transcribed into functional RNA molecules or messenger RNAs destined for translation into a
en.genes_protein_production: The controlled expression of these gene products is strictly necessary for cell survival, proliferation, homeostasis, and specific differentiation.
en.chromosomes_description_p2: Nucleoprotein structures consisting of long strands of DNA stabilized and wrapped around histone cores; they dynamically condense to ensure correct gene segregation during mitotic and meiotic phases.
en.tissues_definition: A tissue is a level of biological organization composed of a consortium of specialized cells sharing embryonic origin, morphological characteristics, and similar functions, integrated and coordinated by means of an extracellular matrix (ECM) of highly variable chemical composition and consistency.
en.tissues_list_intro: The four fundamental biological tissues of the human body are:
en.epithelial_tissue_definition_cellule_add: closely packed and structurally polarized cells
en.epithelial_tissue_definition_spazi_ridot: intercellular extracellular matrix is reduced to narrow clefts
en.epithelial_cell_connections_p1: The mechanical cohesion of the epithelium is ensured by specializations of the lateral plasma membrane that form anchoring, occluding, and communicating junctions, which organize into the
en.epithelial_cell_connections_giunzionale: transmembrane junctional complex stabilized by the cytoskeleton
en.epithelia_types_intro_p1: They are classified into three functional categories:
en.epithelia_types_intro_p3: (specialized in exocrine or endocrine secretory activity), and
en.epithelia_types_intro_p4: (epithelial cells modified for receiving stimuli, e.g., taste buds).
en.epithelium_connective_relationship_intro: Epithelial tissue constantly rests on the
, to which it is anchored through the interposition of the
(an acellular complex structure rich in type IV collagen and laminin). Since epithelial tissue is
en.epithelium_connective_relationship_privo: completely avascular (lacking its own blood vessels)
en.epithelium_connective_relationship_intro: , the exchange of oxygen and nutrients occurs exclusively by passive diffusion across the basement membrane from the capillary beds of the connective tissue.
en.covering_epithelia_layers_intro: Covering epithelia are cytologically classified based on cell morphology and the number of layers:
en.simple_epithelium_details: , composed of a single cell layer resting on the basement membrane;
en.compound_epithelium_details: , consisting of multiple overlapping layers of cells, where only the basal layer touches the membrane;
en.pseudostratified_epithelium_details: , a monostratified variant in which all cells touch the base but, having different heights and nuclear positions, simulate a stratified arrangement on histological section.
en.epithelial_functions_intro_p1: They cover the outer surface of the body (the
en.epithelial_functions_intro_p2: , keratinized epithelial tissue of the skin), the channels and hollow organs communicating with the outside (forming the epithelium of the
en.epithelial_functions_intro_p3: ), the lumens of blood and lymphatic vessels (constituting the
en.epithelial_functions_intro_p4: ), and the internal closed serous cavities and associated organs (constituting the protective
en.factors_determining_function: Cytological differentiation, the number of layers, and the specific anatomical localization strictly condition the functional properties of the epithelium (mechanical protection, selective absorption, secretion, chemical-physical barrier, or molecular transit).
en.glandular_epithelia_definition_p1: Glandular epithelia are formed by cells specialized in anabolism and the regulated secretion of biologically active substances, defined as
en.glandular_epithelia_definition_p2: . These cells can operate as isolated unicellular secretory elements or organize into complex parenchymas that structure the
en.gland_classification_intro: Glands are subdivided and classified mainly based on the method, morphology, and path of extrusion of the secretion:
en.gland_classification_by_secretion: Based on the topography of the secretion release, they are distinguished into:
en.exocrine_glands_details: , which pour their secretion onto the external body surface or into the lumen of a hollow organ, utilizing
en.endocrine_glands_details: , lacking excretory ducts, which release their chemical messengers, defined as
en.endocrine_glands_details_p2: , directly into the interstitial space so that they diffuse into the bloodstream toward distant target organs.
en.connective_tissue_definition_p1: Connective tissue is histologically characterized by the volumetric prevalence of the
en.connective_tissue_definition_p2: over the cellular fraction. The ECM is composed of an
en.connective_tissue_definition_p3: (a water-rich hydrogel containing glycosaminoglycans, proteoglycans, and structural glycoproteins) in which cells and three main varieties of soluble or insoluble
en.connective_tissue_definition_fibre_tipi: collagen fibers (tensile strength), reticular fibers (scaffolding), and elastic fibers (reversible conformation)
en.connective_tissue_subdivisions_intro: Based on density, cell type, and the biochemical composition of the matrix and fibers, connective tissue differentiates into:
en.connective_tissue_list_2: cartilage tissue (solid yet flexible gelled matrix);
en.connective_tissue_functions_intro: Connective tissue fulfills fundamental homeostatic and structural roles:
en.connective_function_1: structural connection and metabolic cohesion between different tissues and organs of the body;
en.connective_function_2: mechanical support of the body (skeletal and cartilaginous framework) and protection of vital organs enclosed within body cavities;
en.connective_function_3: mediation of trophic exchanges and transport of metabolites, ensuring the nutrition of avascular epithelial tissues;
en.connective_function_4: immune response and biological defensive barrier against pathogens and infections (via resident or migrating leukocytes).
en.connective_function_summary: In summary, it performs trophic, mechanical support, protective, and biological defense functions.
en.adipose_tissue_definition_p1: It is a specialized form of loose connective tissue, predominantly composed of cells called
en.adipose_tissue_definition_p2: , responsible for the intracellular accumulation of lipids (mainly triglycerides) in the form of
en.adipose_tissue_types_p1: It is classified into two distinct types:
en.adipose_tissue_types_p2: . White tissue serves as the primary energy reserve of the organism, a thermal insulator, and an endocrine organ; brown tissue is specialized in non-shivering thermogenesis, dissipating the mitochondrial proton gradient into thermal energy (heat) thanks to the expression of the UCP-1 protein (thermogenin).
en.nervous_tissue_definition_p1: Nervous tissue is a highly integrated system composed of excitable cells and associated filaments. The primary functional units are
en.nervous_tissue_definition_p3: , capable of generating, modulating, and transmitting action potentials in response to specific stimuli. The tissue also includes neuroglia (glial cells), essential for the metabolic and structural support of neurons.
en.neuron_structure_p1: Neurons are formed by a cell body (soma or perikaryon) containing the nucleus, from which branched afferent extensions called
en.neuron_structure_p2: emerge, along with a single efferent extension, generally long and myelinated, named
en.neuron_association: Bundles of axons associated with covering connective tissues structure the nerves of the peripheral system.
en.neuron_reproduction_note_p1: Mature neurons are mostly perennial cells permanently entered into the G<sub>0</sub> phase of the cell cycle; however, modern scientific research has demonstrated the existence of phenomena of
en.neuron_reproduction_note_non_si_riproduc: adult neurogenesis starting from neural stem cells confined to specific brain niches (such as the subgranular zone of the hippocampus)
en.neuron_properties_intro: The nerve cell processes and propagates bioelectric impulses based on two fundamental properties:
en.neuron_property_excitability_details: , that is, the capacity to vary the resting membrane potential in response to chemical-physical stimuli, converting them into electrical signals;
en.neuron_property_conductivity_details: , meaning the ability to actively propagate the action potential along the axonal membrane without signal decrement.
en.synapse_description_p1: The unidirectional transmission of information between neurons or between a neuron and an effector cell occurs at specialized junctions called
en.synapse_description_p2: . In chemical synapses, the arrival of the action potential induces the exocytosis of
en.synapse_description_p3: molecules into the synaptic cleft. By binding to receptors on the postsynaptic membrane, these molecules trigger, inhibit, or modulate the nerve impulse, coordinating the integrative activity of the organism.
en._section_dyslexia: === Simple version for dyslexia and low vision  ===
en.cell_definition: The cell is the smallest unit of every living thing. It's like a brick that builds the body.
en.cell_hierarchy_1: Cells join together to form tissues.
en.cell_importance: The human body has many types of cells. They are all important for staying healthy.
en.cell_functions_intro: Cells make the body work. Thanks to them:
en.cell_reproduction: Every cell is born from another cell. It divides into two identical cells. This process is called
en.simple_nucleus_description_1: It's the 'control center' of the cell. It has a double membrane with small holes (pores) to communicate with the rest.
en.simple_nucleus_description_2: Inside are DNA and RNA, which contain genetic information.
en.simple_mitochondrion_description: It's the 'powerhouse'. It produces energy in the form of ATP, which is needed for all cellular functions.
en.simple_golgi_apparatus_description_1: It prepares, modifies, and ships proteins and fats.
en.simple_golgi_apparatus_description_2: It's like the cell's 'post office'. It receives proteins from the endoplasmic reticulum and sends them to the lysosomes or the membrane.
en.simple_er_intro: It's a system of tubes in the cytoplasm. It has two types:
en.simple_smooth_er_description: It has a smooth surface because it doesn't have ribosomes.
en.simple_smooth_er_function_3: Helps the liver clean the blood (detoxification)
en.simple_smooth_er_function_4: Regulates the amount of calcium in the cell
en.simple_rough_er_description_1: It has a 'rough' surface because it's covered in ribosomes.
en.simple_rough_er_description_2: Ribosomes are used to produce proteins. This process is called protein synthesis.
en.simple_peroxisome_description_intro: It's a small structure enclosed by a membrane. It has important functions:
en.simple_peroxisome_function_1: Produces bile acids, which help digest fats
en.simple_peroxisome_function_2: Promotes the absorption of vitamins like A and K
en.simple_peroxisome_function_3: Produces hydrogen peroxide to defend against bacteria
en.simple_peroxisome_function_4: Breaks down long and branched fats
en.simple_peroxisome_function_5: Produces substances (plasmalogens) useful for the brain and lungs
en.simple_lysosome_function_2: Recycles the old parts of the cell (autophagy)
en.simple_lysosome_function_3: Helps white blood cells fight infections
en.simple_lysosome_description_2: Thanks to lysosomes, the cell cleans and regenerates itself.
en.simple_dna_definition: DNA is the molecule that contains the genetic information of every person.
en.simple_dna_components_intro: It's made of many small pieces called nucleotides. Each nucleotide has:
en.simple_dna_component_3: A nitrogenous base (A, T, C, G)
en.simple_dna_genetic_code: The sequence of these bases forms the genetic code, which determines how our body is made and how it works.
en.simple_dna_instructions: DNA gives instructions to other parts of the cell to make the proteins we need.
en.simple_genes_definition_intro: A gene is a piece of DNA that contains the instructions to make:
en.simple_protein_function_3: Its specialization (e.g., muscle cell, nerve cell, etc.)
en.simple_chromosomes_definition: Chromosomes are rod-shaped structures made of DNA and proteins. They are found in the cell's nucleus.
en.simple_chromosomes_mitosis: During cell division (mitosis), the chromosomes copy themselves and move to give each new cell the same genetic makeup.
en.simple_chromosomes_human_number: Humans have 46 chromosomes: 23 inherited from the mother and 23 from the father.
en.simple_chromosomes_uniqueness: Chromosomes contain all the genes that make us unique: from our physical appearance to our body's functions.
